page: Microsoft Azure M832ds_12_v3 is an ultra-high-memory virtual machine belonging to the MDSHighMemoryv3 family. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Intel processors, this server features 832 dedicated vCPUs and a massive memory capacity of 11,400.0 GB. This configuration yields approximately 13.7 GB of RAM per core, positioning it as an ultra-high-density memory instance.

The instance operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It includes a 410 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance external storage volumes. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance, and it does not contain any GPU accelerators.

Given its massive resource footprint, this server represents a highly specialized hardware class designed for extreme scale. The qualitative cost profile reflects its position as a top-tier enterprise resource, where consolidation of massive workloads justifies the infrastructure density. It is engineered for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, massive data warehousing, and complex analytical workloads requiring real-time processing of multi-terabyte datasets.
description: Microsoft Azure M832ds_12_v3 is an ultra-high-memory virtual machine from the MDSHighMemoryv3 family, designed for massive enterprise workloads. Powered by Intel processors on the x86_64 architecture, it features 832 dedicated vCPUs and 11,400.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 13.7 GB of memory per core. The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and includes a 410 GB local disk with Premium Storage capability. It does not feature GPU acceleration or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This server is optimized for extreme resource consolidation and is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise ERP systems, and heavy analytical data processing.

tagline: An ultra-high-density memory instance featuring eight hundred thirty-two dedicated cores and over eleven terabytes of RAM for enterprise workloads.
